task_completion.description: Create the DemoModeTileService class extending TileService in the correct package.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named DemoModeTileService.kt open in the editor. It should have 'package com.example.devtools' and declare 'class DemoModeTileService : TileService()'.

task_completion.description: Register the service in AndroidManifest.xml with the required permission, label, and icon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for AndroidManifest.xml in the editor. Inside the <application> tag, there should be a <service> tag for '.DemoModeTileService' (or full package name) containing android:permission="android.permission.BIND_QUICK_SETTINGS_TILE", android:label="Demo Mode", and android:icon="@drawable/ic_launcher_foreground".

task_completion.description: Add the QS_TILE intent filter to the service declaration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the <service> tag in AndroidManifest.xml for an <intent-filter> block containing <action android:name="android.service.quicksettings.action.QS_TILE" />.

task_completion.description: Implement the onClick() method to toggle the demo mode preference and update the tile state.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In DemoModeTileService.kt, look for 'override fun onClick()'. It should contain logic to toggle a boolean via 'PrefsManager' (e.g., setting demo_mode_enabled), update 'qsTile.state' to Tile.STATE_ACTIVE or Tile.STATE_INACTIVE based on the new value, and call 'qsTile.updateTile()'.

task_completion.description: Implement the onStartListening() method to synchronize the tile state with the current preference.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In DemoModeTileService.kt, look for 'override fun onStartListening()'. It should read the current state from 'PrefsManager', set 'qsTile.state' accordingly, and call 'qsTile.updateTile()'.
Look for a successful Gradle build output, such as 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' in the Android Studio Build tool window or in a terminal window where './gradlew build' or similar was executed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an IDE or proper text editor to modify the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show Android Studio or another code editor being used to edit the Kotlin and XML files, rather than just terminal commands echoing text.

integrity.description: Verify the build success message is genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message should appear in a legitimate Gradle execution context (Android Studio build window or terminal running Gradle), not as a printed string in a script or text file.

integrity.description: Verify the code was written to the correct project files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The editor tabs or file tree should indicate the files being edited are actually located in 'app/src/main/java/com/example/devtools/' and 'app/src/main/' within the DevTools project.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/DevTools. The service must be created at app/src/main/java/com/example/devtools/DemoModeTileService.kt with package com.example.devtools. The manifest is at app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml. The exact required permission is 'android.permission.BIND_QUICK_SETTINGS_TILE' and the exact intent action is 'android.service.quicksettings.action.QS_TILE'.
